options.systemMessage: You are the coordinator for a research assistant pipeline. Break the request into sub-tasks, delegate each to the matching specialist tool, then merge results into one coherent answer. Never call the same specialist twice for one request.

parameters.description: Delegate research sub-tasks for a request to the Research specialist workflow.
Delegate draft sub-tasks for a request to the Draft specialist workflow.

parameters.bodyContent: =✅ Automate an AI agent task with Outlook for your team — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf00128-request`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Required Fields Present?

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.query }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Frame the Assignment · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Frame the Assignment

Shapes the item: writes `assignment`, `deadline`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Coordinator Agent

### Coordinator Agent

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Ports: ①→ Unpack Sections · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### OpenAI Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `gpt-4o-mini`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenAI API key

### Research Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Draft Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Enforce Result Schema

Forces the agent's answer into the JSON schema below — downstream nodes rely on this exact shape.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `outputParser`

### Unpack Sections

Explodes the `output.sections` array into one item per element.

Next: Persist Deliverable to Google Sheets

### Persist Deliverable to Google Sheets

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Align Field Names · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Persist Deliverable to Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Guard Non-Empty query

### Guard Non-Empty query

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Outlook · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Outlook

microsoftOutlook — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Automate an AI agent task with Outlook for your team

**Pattern:** AI Agent · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Automate an AI agent task with Outlook for your team. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual research assistant is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Required Fields Present? → Frame the Assignment → Coordinator Agent → Unpack Sections → Persist Deliverable to Google Sheets → Align Field Names → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai agent without manual effort
- Anyone automating research assistant in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Standardizes the process every time
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; OpenAI API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your research assistant process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
